Interface SpecificProperty<T>

public interface SpecificProperty<T>

Propane generates a custom API for each library using it. Part of this custom API layer is an own "property type" called the libraries specific property type.

This interface is the foundation of this specific property type, which is nothing more than a wrapper around this, like:

public interface JDACProperty<T> extends SpecificProperty<T> {
    ...
}

If you're working with propane or a library using propane, you will mostly see and use the libraries specific property type (like JDACProperty).

Getting the "real" property

This interface doesn't extend Property, thus enabling Property to be sealed and allows the java compiler to check pattern exhaustiveness, which makes working with it easy.

A libraries specific property type must only be implemented by its specific MappingProperty, SingletonProperty and EnumerationProperty, which themselves implement Property. Therefore, a libraries specific property type can always be safely cast to Property.

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    default Property<T>
    generalized()
    Casts this specific property type to Property.
    default T
    scopedGet()
     

  • Method Details

    • generalized

      default Property<T> generalized()
      Casts this specific property type to Property. This cast method is safe as guaranteed by the spec.
      Returns:
      this as a Property

    • scopedGet

      default T scopedGet()
